title: Multiple --platform args don't work with --manifest arg
body:
**Description**

The current documentation states

    The --platform flag can be specified more than once, or given a comma-separated list of values as its argument. When more 
    than one platform is specified, the --manifest option should be used instead of the --tag option.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. `buildah bud --layers --force-rm --jobs=12  --platform=linux/amd64 --platform=linux/battery --platform=linux/correct --platform=linux/horse --platform=linux/staple --manifest=localhost/foo/bar:latest /path/to/context`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
No options (--manifest=localhost/foo/bar:latest) can be specified after the image or container name
time="2021-08-24T15:35:11-04:00" level=error msg="exit status 125"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

An error detailing failure to pull one/more of the (bogus) platform architectures from the base manifest-list (specified in the context dir's `Containerfile` `FROM` command).

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

Compiled from [source](197b9a1fbbe92717c3b48b3c907608774a5dd72)

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah version 1.23.0-dev (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

N/A

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="33 (Workstation Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=33
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f33"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 33 (Workstation Edition)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:33"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/f33/system-administrators-guide/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=33
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=33
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Workstation Edition"
VARIANT_ID=workstation
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux fedora 5.12.17-200.fc33.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Jul 15 12:41:55 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

Stock, from whatever the buildah/podman RPMs are in F33

comments.body:
The same version eventually gives me this error when I point it at `tests/bud/multiarch` in the source tree:
```
4 errors occurred:
	* error creating build container: choosing an image from manifest list docker://alpine:latest: no image found in manifest list for architecture battery, variant "", OS linux
	* error creating build container: choosing an image from manifest list docker://alpine:latest: no image found in manifest list for architecture correct, variant "", OS linux
	* error creating build container: choosing an image from manifest list docker://alpine:latest: no image found in manifest list for architecture horse, variant "", OS linux
	* error creating build container: choosing an image from manifest list docker://alpine:latest: no image found in manifest list for architecture staple, variant "", OS linux
```

What's in your build context and Containerfile that might be triggering different behavior?
Perhaps a log from a run with `--log-level=debug` as the first flag?

> What's in your build context and Containerfile that might be triggering different behavior?

I doubt this is the problem, it's extremely simple `Containerfile`:

```
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal:latest
ENV TESTVER=9.8.7-testing
ADD /output_testver.sh /usr/local/bin
RUN /bin/true
ENTRYPOINT /bin/false
```

> The same version eventually gives me this error

Oh good, that's something more like what I was expecting.  My setup is kind of janky (F33 + compiled buildah).  Let me try again using the `quay.io/buildah/upstream:latest` image...
...confirmed, works correctly, so something in my setup is broken.  Sorry for the noise, I should have thought to try that container image.
